message:
fix(session): gracefully handle unreadable sessions in picker and continue_recent

Previously, if the most recent indexed session file was corrupted or
unreadable, both resume_with_picker() and continue_recent_in_dir()
would propagate the error and fail the entire session startup.

Now:
- continue_recent_in_dir(): iterate through candidate sessions in
  recency order, skipping any that fail to open (with a tracing::warn),
  falling back to creating a fresh session if all candidates fail
- resume_with_picker(): when the user-selected session fails to open,
  warn the user via console and create a fresh session instead of
  propagating the error
- Session save: increase BufWriter capacity to 1MB (1 << 20) for JSONL
  rewrites to reduce write syscall churn on slower disks
- Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] for resume_with_picker
- Format fix for JsonlSaveResult type alias

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
